name: pr_tms_selfloop_plateau
drl:
rule RM when Ord(v > 0) then insertLogical(new M1(0)); end
rule ESC when M1(n < 2) then insertLogical(new M1(1)); end
_finding: The PLATEAU self-loop (external review round 18, case B): the escalation's terminus M1(1) still satisfies the rule's own guard (n < 2), so it justifies ITSELF - a one-node logical cycle. Deleting the grounded root retracts M1(0) but M1(1) survives orphaned, permanently, in BOTH engines (the D-237 JTMS mechanism: justifier sets counted, never grounded). The authoring lint rejects this shape statically (proven-live self-loop); raw DRL keeps the faithful footgun.
